post.description: Exchange the configured `beetkeeper.auth` credentials for a bearer token.
Revoke the bearer token this request authenticated with (identified by the `Authorization` header).

get.description:
Lists the requested page of ingested beets listener events (newest first), each with the beets album/track
ids of its child rows (the ones the beetkeeper event-listener plugin pushed to the POST endpoints).
Returns the list of results for events associated with the beets album ID, if any exist.

schema.title: beets album ID to search listener events by

get.description: Returns the list of results for events associated with the beets item ID, if any exist.

schema.title: beets track ID to search listener events by

get.description: Returns the list of results for events associated with the beetkeeper event item ID, if any exist.

schema.title: beetkeeper event ID to search listener events by

get.description: Report this process's pid, the current import leader (lock holder), and the shared job count.

post.description:
Enqueue an import of the given paths and return the created (PENDING) job.

Set `quiet=true` to import non-interactively (the `beet import -q` equivalent): no decision prompts.
The optional per-job settings (`quiet`, `logpath`, `group_albums`, `flat`, `set_fields`) default to the
corresponding beets config values when left unspecified.

get.description: List one page of known import jobs, newest first (so page 1 shows the most recent submissions).

schema.title: Response List Imports Api Import Get

get.description: Return a single import job (poll this for status / the pending decision).

post.description: Answer the decision an import is parked on; 409 if it isn't awaiting one.
Request cooperative cancellation of an in-flight import.

get.description:
Execute `beet list ...`: return one page of matching tracks (or albums) as JSON objects.

The query params (pagination plus the beets query inputs, some repeatable) are documented on
`ListQueryParamsModel`.

See: https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/v2.12.0/reference/cli.html#list

parameters.name: albums

schema.description: Like `-a`; return albums instead of individual tracks.
schema.title: Albums

parameters.description: Like `-a`; return albums instead of individual tracks.

parameters.name: keyword

schema.description: Repeatable bare-keyword query parts (substring match across default fields).
schema.title: Keyword

parameters.description: Repeatable bare-keyword query parts (substring match across default fields).

parameters.name: field

schema.description: Repeatable `field:value` parts (exact `=`, regex `::`, numeric/date ranges all supported).
schema.title: Field

parameters.description: Repeatable `field:value` parts (exact `=`, regex `::`, numeric/date ranges all supported).

parameters.name: filepath

schema.description: A path within the beets library (becomes a path query).
schema.title: Filepath

parameters.description: A path within the beets library (becomes a path query).

parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description: A beets sort token, e.g. `year+` or `artist-`.
schema.title: Sort By

parameters.description: A beets sort token, e.g. `year+` or `artist-`.

schema.title: Response List  Api Query List Get

get.description:
Execute `beet stats`: track count, total time, approximate size, and artist/album counts.

See: https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/v2.12.0/reference/cli.html#stats

schema.title: Response Stats Api Query Stats Get

get.description:
Execute `beet fields`: the item/album query fields and flexible attributes available.

See: https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/v2.12.0/reference/cli.html#fields

schema.title: Response Fields Api Query Fields Get

APIAlbum.title: APIAlbum
APIAlbum.description:
Model for the relevant parts of a `beets.library.Album` instance. used during beets plugin client push events
for an event tied to a given album. For simplicity, we only mark `id` as required, with all other fields as
optional (which might not be the case for beets internals).

NOTE: This was generated from `build_scripts/code_gen/pydantic_field_info_from_beets_model.py`.

APITrack.title: APITrack
APITrack.description:
Model for the relevant parts of a `beets.library.Item` (track) instance. used during beets plugin client push events
for an event tied to a single track ('Item'). For simplicity, we only mark `id` as required, with all other fields as
optional (which might not be the case for beets internals).

NOTE: This was generated from `build_scripts/code_gen/pydantic_field_info_from_beets_model.py`.

BeetsEventType.title: BeetsEventType
BeetsEventType.description:
Subset of beets listener `event_type`s which the API accepts from our plugin client.
See also:
    https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/dev/plugins/events.html

DecisionRequest.title: DecisionRequest
DecisionRequest.description: Published when the importer needs a user choice for a task; the matching `ImportDecision` unblocks it.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Any error messages from the event search.

EventSearchResponse.title: EventSearchResponse
EventSearchResponse.description: Response model for the `GET /api/events/(album|track)/{id}` and `GET /api/events/{event_id}` search routes.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: The beetkeeper event ID associated with this match.

event_time.title: Event Time
event_time.description: The time of the event taking place.

beets_id.title: Beets Id
beets_id.description: The beets ID of the event's subject (either beets Album ID or Item ID).

current_beets_subject_state.title: Current Beets Subject State
current_beets_subject_state.description: Current state of the event's subject (Album or Item). Null if subject no longer exists.

EventSearchResult.title: EventSearchResult
EventSearchResult.description: A model with an aggregate of details for an event search match.

EventsListResponse.title: EventsListResponse
EventsListResponse.description: Response payload for the events listing: recently ingested listener events, newest first.

ImportAction.title: ImportAction
ImportAction.description: beetkeeper-side mirror of beets' import `action` choices (decoupled from beets internals).

album_id.title: Album Id
album_id.description: Source-specific release id (e.g. a MusicBrainz MBID).

release_url.title: Release Url
release_url.description: Web URL for the release, from the source plugin (beets' `AlbumInfo.data_url`).

details.title: Details
details.description: One-line summary of the differentiating attributes (empty string when none are known).

ImportCandidate.title: ImportCandidate
ImportCandidate.description:
A serializable view of one beets match candidate, surfaced to the UI for selection.

Beyond the headline `label`/`similarity`, the optional release attributes below let the UI tell
otherwise-identical candidates apart (e.g. different pressings/editions of the same album); `details`
joins the populated ones into a single human-readable line for display.

ImportDecision.title: ImportDecision
ImportDecision.description: The user's answer to a `DecisionRequest` (posted back from the UI).

source_label.title: Source Label
source_label.description: Display name for the import source: the basename(s) of the path(s) (e.g. the album folder).

ImportJob.title: ImportJob
ImportJob.description: API-facing view of a single import job (the persisted record is `db.models.ImportJobRecord`).

ImportJobStatus.title: ImportJobStatus
ImportJobStatus.description: Lifecycle states of an import job.

paths.title: Paths
paths.description: One or more paths (files/dirs) to import.

quiet.title: Quiet
quiet.description: Run non-interactively (like `beet import -q`): never prompt. Strong matches are applied automatically; anything else falls back to beets' `quiet_fallback` config (skip by default).

logpath.title: Logpath
logpath.description: Corresponds to the `-l` option when running an import command, or config's `import.log` option. See https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference/cli.html#import

flat.title: Flat
flat.description: Corresponds to the `--flat` flag when running an import command. See https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference/cli.html#import

set_fields.title: Set Fields
set_fields.description: Corresponds to the `--set field=value` option for beets' `import` CLI command. Can set multiple key-value pairs.

ImportSubmitRequest.title: ImportSubmitRequest
ImportSubmitRequest.description:
Body for starting an import: the filesystem path(s) beets should import. All default values come from the
`beets.config` values (cached) if left unspecified.

ListenerEventDetails.title: ListenerEventDetails
ListenerEventDetails.description: One ingested beets listener event, with the beets album/track ids of its child rows.

LoginResponseBody.title: LoginResponseBody
LoginResponseBody.description:
The raw bearer token the client must send back as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (`str`, not
`SecretStr` — this response is the one place the token is intentionally revealed).

tags.name: authentication
tags.description:
Used only when optional "beetkeeper.auth.enable_login_protection" is "true". 
Routes for simple login / logout flows. Beetkeeper does not support multiple user identities.
Only handles authentication, with implicit authorization to everything for an authenticated client.

tags.name: imports
tags.description:
Routes for managing [beets imports](https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference/cli.html#import).
Offer API-based import execution, import history, import choice selection, and more.
For automated beets setups, you will typically rely on these endpoints the most. 
These endpoints also power the frontend import management web UI page.

tags.name: query
tags.description:
Routes for [querying your beets library](https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference/query.html).
Supports API-based queries, using standard `beets` query syntax. Useful for checking the status of your
beets-managed library. These endpoints also power the frontend query UI page.

tags.name: events
tags.description:
Routes pertaining to [beets events](https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/dev/plugins/events.html).
Documented routes read-only. The [beetkeeper-plugin library](https://pypi.org/project/beetkeeper-plugin/)
automatically pushes beets events to the beetkeeper server. Events serve to provide a full historical
trace of any / all beets imports, file operations, etc. Helpful for tracing origins, or fine-tuning beets
configurations, especially in automated setups.

tags.name: monitoring
tags.description: Routes for beetkeeper system observability, and availability checks.
